title: My new Tablet PC
body: At first, I hadn't planned on buying a touchscreen laptop. What I was looking for was a laptop that was truly easy to carry, lightweight, 11 to 14 inches at most, and had good connectivity (Wi-Fi and Bluetooth). I also consider it essential that it have a webcam and a card reader, as well as a good hard drive capacity so I can download photos and videos from my digital camera or phone. And of course, I wanted all this at a good price, around €1,000. I searched quite a bit; the market doesn’t have many computers with small screens, and the ones that are available (Sony Vaio, Asus, Acer) were a bit over my budget. The Apple iBook seemed like a good option to me, but the model with a DVD burner and good hard drive capacity also exceeded my budget. In the end, after visiting many websites and a few stores, I decided on the [HP Pavilion tx1250 from the tx1200 series](http://h10010.www1.hp.com/wwpc/es/es/ho/WF25a/21955-186135-315641-315641-315641-80246421.html), which for €999 offers everything I need and also has a touchscreen and fingerprint reader, plus it looks pretty good. The only downsides I see are that it only has 1GB of RAM and that the screen, being a touchscreen, isn’t the best. I seem to have partially solved the memory issue by using a 2GB SD card as ReadyBoost, a feature offered by Windows Vista. Otherwise, I’m pretty happy with it. ![HP Pavilion tx1200 series](http://farm2.static.flickr.com/1366/1332907068_5bc8ae0a37.jpg?v=0 "HP Pavilion tx1200 series") ![HP Pavilion tx1200 series](http://farm2.static.flickr.com/1372/1332906504_615b1ba359.jpg?v=0 "HP Pavilion tx1200 series")
